title: dortmanna senegalensis
intro: Dortmanna senegalensis, also known as the Fever Nut or African fever tree, is a medicinal plant native to West Africa. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antimalarial properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties, providing relief from fever, pain, and respiratory issues, as well as offering antioxidant and anti-cancer benefits. In herbal preparations, the roots of the plant are typically used to make teas, infusions, or extracts, which can be taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
